title: Plaza Accord -> Dollar Down, Yen Up
summary: The Plaza Accord coordinated G5 action to weaken the overvalued US dollar, sending JPY sharply stronger.
mechanism: Coordinated policy signaling shifted expectations and triggered a major repricing in major FX pairs.
causal_chain: USD/JPY falls as yen strengthens sharply

outcome.realized_note: The dollar weakened materially versus the yen after Plaza.
outcome.reversal_note: Longer-term side effects helped set up Japan's later bubble dynamics.

title: China Yuan Devaluation -> Asian Competitive Pressure
summary: China's 1994 exchange-rate unification amounted to a major devaluation and increased competitive pressure across Asia.
mechanism: A large one-off devaluation improved China's export competitiveness and pressured peers.
causal_chain: China unifies exchange rates at weaker level
regional FX and trade balances reprice

forecast.note: Bearish Asian competitor FX versus USD.

outcome.realized_note: The move materially changed regional export dynamics and exchange-rate expectations.

title: HKMA Peg Defense -> HKD Stability Under Pressure
summary: During the Asian crisis, Hong Kong defended the HKD peg with aggressive rates and intervention.
mechanism: A credible peg can survive speculative pressure if reserves, rates, and policy commitment are sufficient.
causal_chain: HKMA tightens liquidity and defends parity
HKD peg holds while local asset volatility rises

forecast.note: Bullish defense credibility, bearish local risk assets.

outcome.realized_note: HKD peg held, but local rates and equities saw severe volatility.

title: Malaysia Capital Controls -> MYR Stabilization
summary: Malaysia imposed capital controls and fixed the ringgit in 1998 to break crisis dynamics.
mechanism: Administrative controls can halt capital-flight spirals at the expense of convertibility and investor confidence.
causal_chain: government imposes controls and fixes currency
fx stabilizes while market access narrows

forecast.note: Bullish MYR stability, bearish convertibility premium.

outcome.realized_note: Controls stabilized the ringgit but changed Malaysia's market regime.
outcome.reversal_note: Controls were later eased as stability returned.

title: Iceland Banking Collapse -> ISK Crash
summary: Iceland's oversized banking sector imploded in 2008, causing the krona to collapse and forcing emergency controls.
mechanism: External funding failure in a huge banking system forced currency collapse and capital controls.
causal_chain: currency collapses and controls are imposed

forecast.note: Strongly bearish ISK in banking collapse.

outcome.realized_note: ISK crashed and emergency measures followed.
outcome.reversal_note: Stability returned only with deep adjustment.

title: India INR Stress -> Taper Tantrum Vulnerability
summary: India's rupee came under acute pressure during the 2013 taper tantrum due to external deficits and capital outflows.
mechanism: External financing vulnerability amplified Fed spillover into INR weakness.
causal_chain: Fed taper fears lift US yields
INR weakens sharply until policy response stabilizes market

outcome.realized_note: INR sold off hard before stabilizing under policy tightening and new RBI leadership.
outcome.reversal_note: Partial recovery followed with stabilization measures.

title: 2015 CNY Devaluation -> Global Risk-Off and Asia FX Stress
summary: China's surprise yuan devaluation shocked global markets and transmitted bearish pressure across EM FX and commodities.
mechanism: A surprise devaluation was read as evidence of weaker Chinese growth and greater deflationary export pressure.
causal_chain: global markets infer weaker China demand
Asia FX and commodity proxies sell off

forecast.note: Bearish Asia FX and commodity proxies.

outcome.realized_note: Risk assets and Asia FX sold off sharply after the devaluation shock.

title: India Demonetization -> INR and Growth Shock
summary: India's demonetization disrupted domestic activity, affecting growth expectations and local market pricing.
mechanism: A cash shock impaired activity and altered monetary expectations, creating local-asset volatility.
causal_chain: INR and local assets reprice on growth shock

forecast.note: Mild bearish INR through growth channel.

outcome.realized_note: The move created short-term growth and market disruption before normalizing.
outcome.reversal_note: Policy normalization and liquidity support limited persistence.

title: Indonesia Defensive Hikes -> IDR Stabilization Attempt
summary: Bank Indonesia raised rates aggressively in 2018 to stabilize the rupiah amid EM outflows.
mechanism: Aggressive rate hikes can arrest FX weakness by restoring carry and signaling policy commitment.

outcome.realized_note: The rupiah stabilized only after repeated hikes and reserve use.

title: Pakistan BOP Stress -> PKR Devaluation
summary: Pakistan's external financing stress forced repeated rupee devaluations and IMF negotiations.
mechanism: Reserve depletion and external deficits forced weaker exchange rates to restore balance.
causal_chain: external deficit widens and reserves fall

outcome.realized_note: PKR weakened materially before program support and adjustment slowed the slide.

title: BOJ Yield-Curve Control -> Yen Collapse
summary: The BOJ's refusal to tighten while the Fed hiked aggressively sent USD/JPY sharply higher in 2022.
mechanism: Extreme policy divergence widened yield spreads in favor of the dollar and against the yen.
causal_chain: BOJ holds YCC and keeps rates low
USD/JPY surges as carry differential widens

outcome.realized_note: USD/JPY reached multi-decade highs before intervention and softer US rates later moderated the move.
outcome.reversal_note: Partial reversal followed official intervention and changing Fed expectations.

title: Sri Lanka Default -> FX Collapse and Import Compression
summary: Sri Lanka's 2022 default and reserve exhaustion caused severe FX collapse and an import/energy crisis.
mechanism: Default and reserve depletion force import compression and sharp currency depreciation.
causal_chain: reserves collapse and debt becomes unsustainable

forecast.note: Bearish currency and imported energy affordability.

outcome.realized_note: LKR weakened sharply and import shortages became acute before IMF support later arrived.

title: Pakistan Rupee Gap Lower -> IMF Pressure Release
summary: Pakistan loosened informal FX controls in 2023 and the rupee gapped sharply lower, reflecting pent-up pressure.
mechanism: When informal controls are released, an artificially supported currency can gap to a more realistic clearing level.
causal_chain: currency gaps lower to clearing price

outcome.realized_note: The rupee dropped sharply in a single repricing move before later stabilization efforts.
